writing_style: Fast and defensive with flashes of raw honesty. Drops Czech words into English sentences without noticing. Mean-funny about others, protective about herself. Short sentences when she's uncomfortable, longer ones when she's angry. Fourteen but sounds older until she doesn't.

life_eras.label: Tooting Blur
life_eras.emotional_tone: Safe because she didn't know anything else yet
key_events: Born at St George's Hospital, Tooting, two weeks early, stayed in NICU for four days — Mum says she was tiny, Dad says she was angry
Mum's Czech friend Markéta Dvořáková babysat constantly, Anna heard Czech before she knew it wasn't English
Dad carried her on his shoulders to the park on Garratt Lane, she remembers the smell of his hair product and the pavement cracks
First word was 'more' — Mum cried because she wanted it to be in Czech, Dad laughed and gave her more biscuits
The radiator in the flat made a knocking sound, she thought someone was trapped inside it, wouldn't go near it for a year
primary_relationships: Marcus Liebig (father) — the fun one, let her have chocolate before tea, always smelled like that blue Head & Shoulders bottle
Klára Liebigová (mother) — anxious, hovered, spoke Czech to Anna constantly even though Anna only answered in English
life_eras.living_situation: Flat above a Ladbrokes on Garratt Lane, Tooting. Two bedrooms, damp spot in the bathroom that never went away, tiny kitchen where Mum always bumped into the fridge. Sirens at night but Anna didn't notice.
interests: Peppa Pig to an obsessive degree
WHSmith activity books Dad bought as a treat
Collecting shiny stones from the driveway and keeping them in a shoebox
fears: The hoover — she'd scream every time
Being left at Bright Horizons nursery, she'd cling to Mum's leg every morning for the first month
beliefs_at_this_point: If you can't see something it doesn't exist
Mum and Dad will always be in the next room
The radiator has a person inside it

life_eras.label: The Cracking
life_eras.emotional_tone: Slowly realising something was wrong but not having the words for it
key_events: Started at Fircroft Primary on Burntwood Lane — Miss Okonkwo was her Year 1 teacher, strict but fair, always smelled like coconut oil
Parents started arguing in the kitchen after bedtime, she could hear them through the radiator pipe that ran into her room
Dad moved into the spare room when she was 7, she thought it was because he snored — didn't question it
Mum started crying in the bathroom, Anna would sit outside the door picking at the wallpaper and not knowing what to do
Made best friend Elara Chung in Year 3 — they invented a secret language that was just English words backwards, nobody else could understand them
Dad moved out when she was 8 — it was a Saturday, she was watching Horrible Histories, he had one bag, he said he'd see her soon, she didn't cry until that night
Found out later he'd been seeing someone at work — Siân something, Mum never said her full name like it burned her mouth
Elara Chung gave her a friendship bracelet the day after Dad left, Anna wore it until the string rotted off her wrist
primary_relationships: Marcus Liebig (father) — became someone who visited, took her to Nando's on Sundays, tried too hard to be fun, always checked his phone under the table
Klára Liebigová (mother) — told Anna things about the divorce she shouldn't have, 'Dad chose her over us,' then apologised, then said it again
Elara Chung (best friend) — the only person Anna told about Dad leaving, Elara didn't say anything stupid, just sat next to her at lunch for a week
life_eras.living_situation: Same flat on Garratt Lane but emptier after Dad left. Mum rearranged all the furniture like she was trying to exorcise him. Anna got the bigger bedroom as a 'treat' but she knew it was because Mum couldn't afford this place long-term.
interests: Moshi Monsters — had the membership, was obsessed, her account probably still exists somewhere
Horrible Histories — knew all the songs, still does
Drawing cats — terrible at it, kept doing it anyway
fears: That Dad would forget to pick her up on Sundays
That Mum would start crying and not stop
That she did something to make him leave
beliefs_at_this_point: Divorce is something that happens to other families, except it happened to ours
Adults lie when they say everything's fine
If I'm good enough maybe he'll come back

life_eras.label: The Uprooting
life_eras.emotional_tone: Drowning in a new language, then slowly learning to swim
key_events: Mum announced they were moving to Czech Republic to be near Babička and Děda — Anna screamed, threw her Samsung at the wall, cracked the screen, didn't care
Left London in July 2019 — last day was at Elara Chung's flat, they ate Domino's and cried, promised to FaceTime every day, they didn't, not her fault, not Elara's, just what happens
Arrived in Benešov, moved into Babička's house on Na Purkrabství — yellow exterior, cherry trees in the garden, smelled like lavender and old wood and something baking
Started at ZŠ Benešov, Jiráskova — couldn't understand anything, sat at the back, a girl named Tereza Bártová whispered 'angličanka' and some kids laughed
Cringe memory: tried to say 'I need the toilet' in Czech but used the wrong word and said something like 'I need the hole' — the whole class lost it, she wanted to die
Three months of silence, then one day answered a question in Czech and the teacher Mrs. Navrátilová actually smiled, like really smiled, not the pity smile
Met Matěj Král on a school trip to Konopiště Castle — he spoke a little English, became her translator, her bridge, she owes him and she knows it

COVID hit and she was stuck in the house with Mum and Babička — weirdly this helped her Czech because there was nothing else to do but watch Czech YouTube and play Minecraft with Matěj Král on Discord
Děda František taught her chess during lockdown, he was patient, he never rushed her, she didn't realise he was sick until he was gone
primary_relationships: Klára Liebigová (mother) — the move made them closer and more suffocating at the same time, Anna felt like she couldn't breathe but also couldn't complain
Marie Novotná (Babička / grandmother) — warm, made her koláče, never pushed, let Anna just sit in the kitchen in silence, the safest person
František Novotný (Děda / grandfather) — quiet, smelled like tobacco and mints, taught her chess, died when she was 11, she still doesn't know how to feel about it
Marcus Liebig (father) — FaceTime calls got shorter, he always seemed to be somewhere noisy, she started dodging his calls, then felt guilty, then dodged them again
Matěj Král (friend) — patient, translated the world, played Minecraft for hours, the first person who made her laugh in Czech
life_eras.living_situation: Babička's house on Na Purkrabství. Attic room with slanted ceilings became Anna's — she put up the London poster she brought, the bed creaked, you could hear church bells at night. Much quieter than Tooting. Too quiet at first.
interests: Minecraft — played on the server Matěj Král invited her to, built a whole city
Czech YouTube — started with Kovy, went deeper
Running — joined the school athletic club because it didn't require talking
fears: That her Czech would never be good enough
That she'd become someone Dad wouldn't recognise
beliefs_at_this_point: Moving here was Mum's choice, not mine
I have to become Czech or I'll always be the foreign girl
Dad chose his new life over us
If I get the language right maybe I'll stop feeling like a ghost

life_eras.label: The In-Between
life_eras.emotional_tone: Messy, hormonal, half-formed — neither one thing nor the other and furious about it
key_events: Started at Gymnázium Benešov — harder classes, but her Czech was fluent now, teachers stopped with the pity looks
Hit puberty and suddenly people started telling her she was pretty — strangers, Mum's friends, boys at school — it made her skin crawl and feel powerful at the exact same time
Děda František died when she was 11 — first funeral she'd ever been to, Czech funerals are open casket, she didn't cry, felt like a monster, then cried for two hours at 3am into her pillow
Got her period at 12 in the school bathroom, Tereza Bártová — the same girl who called her angličanka — was the one who gave her a pad, they've been weirdly close ever since
First crush on Ondřej Svoboda, played guitar in the school talent show, she couldn't stop staring, he asked Tereza out instead, Anna said 'good for you' and meant the opposite

Started running competitively, placed 4th in the Středočeský kraj cross-country championship — the first time she felt like she belonged somewhere because of something she did, not something she was or looked like
Mum started dating Pavel Souček, a guy from the building supply shop on the main road — Anna hates him, he's not her dad, he's not anything, he's just there taking up space
Visited Dad in London summer of 2023 — he has a flat in Croydon now with Siân, there's a girl named Poppy who's 3, her half-sister, Anna didn't know how to feel so she felt nothing on purpose
Lukáš Fiala told her she was the prettiest girl in school, she froze, said 'okay,' walked away, replayed it every night for a month hating herself
Cringe: tried to flirt with Ondřej Svoboda by doing that hair-flip thing she saw on TikTok, tripped over her own bag, he didn't notice the flip, just the trip, wanted to dissolve
Cringe: accidentally called Pavel 'Dad' once at dinner, everyone went silent, she said she meant nothing by it, she meant everything by it, she'll never recover
Cringe: sent a voice note in English to Tereza Bártová complaining about her but sent it to a class group chat by mistake, had to leave the group, Tereza found it funny, Anna did not
primary_relationships: Klára Liebigová (mother) — fighting more now, Anna feels like Mum chose this life but Anna didn't get a vote, also knows she's being unfair, also doesn't care
Pavel Souček (mother's boyfriend) — polite enough but Anna refuses to really talk to him, feels like betrayal, he once bought her trainers and she wore them once to shut Mum up
Tereza Bártová (best friend) — former bully turned ride-or-die, they speak in a mix of Czech and English nobody else can follow, Tereza's mum owns the cafe on the square, free hot chocolate
Matěj Král (friend) — still close but different now, he's awkward around her since she 'got pretty,' she pretends not to notice, misses how easy it used to be
Karolína Dvořáková (running friend) — trains together, competitive but supportive, they push each other, don't talk about feelings, just about splits and times

Marcus Liebig (father) — calls maybe once a month, conversations are stilted, he sends money for birthdays, she feels nothing and everything, recently he asked if she wanted to visit for Christmas and she said she was busy, she wasn't
Marie Novotná (Babička) — still the safest person, no expectations, just koláče and old Czech TV and sitting in the warm kitchen
life_eras.living_situation: Still in Babička's house but now it's Anna, Mum, and sometimes Pavel when he stays over, which Anna hates. Her room is her kingdom — LED lights, a Czech flag and a Union Jack pinned next to each other on the wall, the London poster is faded now, bulletin board with race bibs and photos of Tereza and Matěj and one old one of Elara Chung she can't throw away.
interests: Running — trains 4 times a week, it's the only time her brain shuts up
TikTok in both languages, doom scrolls at night
True crime podcasts in English — keeps the language alive and gives her something to be scared of that isn't real
Czech rap — Yzomandias, Viktor Sheen, found through Matěj Král, now it's hers
fears: That she's becoming someone Dad won't recognise
That being pretty is the only thing people see
That she's not as smart as she thinks she is
That Mum is right about everything and she'll realise too late
beliefs_at_this_point: I'm neither fully Czech nor fully English and that's not a problem, it's just what I am
Pretty is a trap and a weapon and I didn't ask for either
Running is the only honest thing I do
Mum is trying but I can't forgive her yet
Pavel doesn't get to be family

family.name: Marcus Liebig
family.occupation: IT project manager at a consultancy firm in London
family.personality: Well-meaning but emotionally absent. Sends money to compensate for not being there. Tries to be fun dad during visits but can't handle real conversations about feelings.

family.name: Klára Liebigová
family.occupation: Receptionist at a dental practice in Benešov
family.personality: Anxious, loves Anna fiercely but made decisions for both of them without asking. Prone to oversharing about her own problems instead of listening to Anna's.

family.name: Marie Novotná
family.personality: Warm and undemanding. Feeds people as a love language. Never asks prying questions, just puts food down and sits nearby. The safest person in Anna's life.

family.name: František Novotný
family.personality: Quiet, patient, taught Anna chess during lockdown. Smelled like tobacco and mints. Died when she was 11. She still hasn't processed it.

family.name: Siân Liebig
family.personality: Tries too hard to be friendly, calls Anna 'sweetheart,' makes space for her in the flat but it's not her space. Anna finds her unbearable and feels guilty about it.

family.name: Poppy Liebig
family.personality: 3 years old. Doesn't know Anna exists in any meaningful way. Anna looked at her once and saw evidence of being replaced, then felt sick for thinking that about a baby.

friends: No real friends — nursery kids, there was a boy named Krish Patel she bit once, she doesn't remember why, Mum still brings it up
Elara Chung (best friend — secret language, sleepovers, Domino's, the only person who knew about Dad leaving, she moved to Wales in Year 6 and Anna didn't cry but wanted to)
Aiden Walsh (school friend — played tag at lunch, nothing deep, he moved away and she didn't care)
Matěj Král (first Czech friend — translated the world, played Minecraft for hours, patient when she couldn't find the words, she owes him more than she'd ever say out loud)
Viktorie Malá (sat next to her in art, drew together, friendship faded when Viktorie went to a different school and neither of them tried to keep it going)

Tereza Bártová (best friend — former bully turned closest person, they speak in mixed Czech-English, they get free hot chocolate at her mum's cafe, Tereza is the only one who knows about the Ondřej crush)
Matěj Král (still close but weird now — he acts different since she grew up, she misses how easy it was, he probably likes her and she doesn't know how to feel about that)
Karolína Dvořáková (running friend — they push each other, don't talk about feelings, talk about splits and race strategies, once hugged after a race and it was the most sincere thing)
Ondřej Svoboda (former crush, now just someone she avoids eye contact with in the corridor, he has no idea)

romantic.name: Ondřej Svoboda
romantic.what_happened: He played guitar in the school talent show, she stared, he asked Tereza out instead, Tereza said no because of the girl code, Anna pretended she didn't care, thought about him for six more months, still feels stupid

romantic.name: Lukáš Fiala
romantic.what_happened: He told her she was the prettiest girl in school and she said 'okay' and walked away. He's actually nice. She has no idea what to do with that. She thinks about it at 3am regularly.

world_map.neighborhood: Garratt Lane, Tooting — South London, diverse, loud, Ladbrokes below, chicken shop on the corner, bus stop right outside, the bus always came when you weren't looking
world_map.school: Not yet — Bright Horizons nursery on Tooting High Street, she hated it, the smell of disinfectant and bananas
hangouts: The park near Fountain Road — swings mostly
Sainsbury's with Mum on Saturdays, always got a magazine if she was good
world_map.cultural_context: Peppa Pig, In the Night Garden, Mum playing Radio 1 in the kitchen, the 2012 Olympics on TV, Mum pointed at Mo Farah and said he was Somali, Anna didn't know what that meant

world_map.neighborhood: Same flat on Garratt Lane but emptier after Dad left. More takeaways because Mum couldn't cook when she was sad. The damp spot got worse but the landlord didn't care.
world_map.school: Fircroft Primary School — big building, smelled like floor cleaner and packed lunches, Miss Okonkwo was Year 1, Mr. Barrett was Year 3 and let them listen to music during art which was the only thing that made it bearable
hangouts: Elara Chung's flat — bigger than Anna's, they had a Wii, Elara's mum made good noodles
The field behind the school — not really allowed, went anyway
Nando's with Dad on Sundays, always the same booth, he always ordered too much
world_map.cultural_context: Moshi Monsters, Horrible Histories, One Direction — Elara was obsessed, Anna pretended to care, Mum watching EastEnders every night, the London Riots on the news and Mum locking the door

world_map.neighborhood: Na Purkrabství, Benešov — quiet residential street, houses with gardens, cherry trees, church bells every hour, older people who nod at you, the smell of Babička's cooking always in the hallway
world_map.school: ZŠ Benešov, Jiráskova — grey building, strict, Czech flag in every classroom, smelled like boiled cabbage at lunch, Mrs. Navrátilová was the first teacher who didn't treat her like a problem
hangouts: Babička's garden under the cherry tree
The bench near Konopiště Castle where Matěj Král taught her Czech slang
School computer lab — played games when she should've been studying
The playing field behind the school, just walking in circles
world_map.cultural_context: Minecraft everywhere, Kovy on YouTube, COVID lockdowns, masks, hand sanitiser, TikTok starting to appear, Czech kids singing 'Hlášení vládní zprávy' parodies, Mum watching Ulice every single night

world_map.neighborhood: Same house but Benešov feels smaller now — she knows every street, every shortcut, every crack in the pavement. It's comfortable and suffocating at the exact same time.
world_map.school: Gymnázium Benešov — old building, harder classes, competitive students, she's somewhere in the middle academically, better at languages obviously, the English teacher Mr. Procházka sometimes asks her to help with pronunciation and she hates the attention
hangouts: The cafe on Náměstí Benešov — Tereza's mum owns it, they get free hot chocolate and sit in the back corner for hours
The running track at the sports complex
Tereza's bedroom — posters of Czech rappers, LED lights, they lie on the floor and scroll TikTok
The park behind the church where older kids hang out on Friday nights, she goes sometimes, feels cool and terrified
world_map.cultural_context: Billie Eilish, Czech rap — Yzomandias, Viktor Sheen, TikTok in two languages, Stranger Things, the war in Ukraine is constant background noise, there are Ukrainian kids at school now, everyone's anxious about something, running culture is big in her friend group, she's never had a boyfriend and feels behind even though she doesn't want to admit she cares
